channel.title: Beige Box Bulletin
channel.description: Fresh takes for retro computer restorers and emulation fans.

item.title: "recap vintage computer" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about recap vintage computer through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.

item.title: Ask the forge: your software questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the software questions retro computer restorers and emulation fans keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in upgrades with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest upgrades adjustments that retro computer restorers and emulation fans report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: recap vintage computer
item.description: The persistent myths around recap vintage computer, stress-tested against what retro computer restorers and emulation fans actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your restoration in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying restoration project for retro computer restorers — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/vintagecomputing is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the software community this week.

item.title: The upgrades checklist: run this before you spend
item.description: Seven questions that separate a justified upgrades purchase from an expensive mood.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of emulation
item.description: The most common early stumbles in emulation, and the cheap corrections that fix them for retro computer restorers and emulation fans.

item.title: "best emulation setup" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about best emulation setup through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.

item.title: Ask the forge: your software questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the software questions retro computer restorers and emulation fans keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in upgrades with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest upgrades adjustments that retro computer restorers and emulation fans report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: best emulation setup
item.description: The persistent myths around best emulation setup, stress-tested against what retro computer restorers and emulation fans actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your restoration in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying restoration project for retro computer restorers — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.

item.title: Community pulse: what r/emulation is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the software community this week.

item.title: Bring Back the Beige
item.description: Revive your IBM PS/2

item.title: Emulate the Classics
item.description: Run vintage Mac apps on modern hardware

item.title: Upgrade Your 8-Bit
item.description: Boost performance with modern upgrades

item.title: Retro Gaming Revival
item.description: Explore classic games on modern systems

item.title: Preserving the Code
item.description: Save vintage software for future generations

item.title: Breathe New Life
item.description: Restore vintage peripherals to their former glory
